content:
(Pros, Cons & Best Use Cases)

3 min read
Just now

--

Building a website has never been easier. Whether you’re creating a personal portfolio, a business website, a blog, or your next SaaS application, deploying it online no longer requires expensive hosting. Today, several cloud platforms offer generous free tiers that let developers publish websites and web applications in just a few minutes.
In this article, we’ll explore 12 of the best free website deployment platforms, their strengths, limitations, and the types of projects they’re best suited for.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Made with Gemini1. Vercel
Best for: Next.js, React, frontend applications
Vercel is one of the most popular deployment platforms for modern web development. Built by the creators of Next.js, it offers automatic deployments from GitHub, preview deployments for every pull request, global edge delivery, and free SSL certificates.
Pros

• Lightning-fast deployments

• Automatic Git integration

• Global CDN

• Preview deployments

• Free custom domains
Cons

• Free tier has usage limits

• Primarily designed for…
